item.title: Empathy for money
item.description: Fp: I want to say thank you for everybody who helped or followed my wife and I and our journey from Virginia to minnesota. We are starting to land on our feet and be able to move forward. As a disabled combat veteran, I want to say thank you for everybody who voted for Harris, and everybody who is supporting what this country is actually supposed to be. Trying to move from Tiktok to YouTube. Would appreciate a subscribe. https://youtube.com/@grid.squares?si=cSvuvjp3Hpy1cJ0q
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares

item.title: Hands off Spokane Wa had a good turn out today
item.description:
Some people dressed up
Someone gifted me an extra sign they made
Some people turned up (about 2,000 I think?)
Some people chanted
Some really great protest music was played But most importantly, Everybody was standing together

item.title: IQ tests are BS
item.description: So here's a little story of my experience with IQ tests. Back in high school the school had to have me take a proctored IQ test due to me having an IEP. They brought in my parents and me to go over the results, because according to the test I was literally 2 points shy of "genius". Thing is. Back then I sucked at math. I cannot stress it enough just how bad I was at math. I had to retake pre-algebra SOOO many times. It wasn't until I was in college and took an algebra course tailored towards those with difficulty doing math that I was finally able to start doing decent at it. Anyways, back in high school people would always ride me about "why aren't you getting this when the test says you're so smart?" They all saw the results of a flawed test of intelligence and expected me to be great at all academic matters.
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.title: Duval County Jacksonville Florida Showed Up Today to Fight for Our Democracy!
item.description:
Standing side by side with my fellow citizens really did a lot for my mental health today.
We showed up to stand for a country not lost but fighting for it's future against the tyranny of a fascist president and government.
My friends and neighbors are much better than me at making signs. I love my community and neighbors and will fight for equal rights for all and for my friends and neighbors to be able to be their true selves and to feel safe being themselves.
United we can stand up against this tragedy that's happening in our country right now.
Together we can save our children's future.
Get out and be a part of the movement to stop Trump and Musk.
It's an amazing feeling being together. Saying I love you neighbor, I care about your life. Your problems are my problems. I will fight.
Show up America. We can fight this if we stand together.
item.dc:creator: hordesofgourds

item.title: This beautiful species
item.description: Dik-dik (Madoqua kirkii) "When frightened or disturbed, they make a whistling sound through their nose that sounds like “zik-zik,” and this is probably how they got their name." Source: https://www.awf.org/wildlife-conservation/dik-dik

item.title: Pork Pappardelle with Tarragon Recipe!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/pork-pappardelle-with-tarragon/ I had a string of very bad meals that all turned out HORRIBLY, but this one was my redemption dinner!! It's SO good!! :) 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil 1 pound ground pork 1 yellow onion, peeled and small-diced 1 tablespoon Italian seasoning 1 teaspoon crushed red pepper, more or less to taste 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1 pint cherry tomatoes 1 zucchini, about 8 ounces, trimmed and small-diced 2 teaspoons Dijon mustard 1½ cups chicken stock 2 teaspoons tarragon, minced ½ cup parsley, loosely packed, minced; plus more for garnish 1 lemon, juiced 12 ounces pappardelle, or pasta of choice Salt and pepper to taste Brown the pork: Heat 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Once hot, add the pork and cook for 8 to 10 minutes, breaking it up as it cooks. Add the yellow onion and continue cooking for 5 to 8 minutes until the pork is cooked through and the onion softens. Season with 1 teaspoon kosher salt and black pepper to taste. Add Italian seasoning and crushed red pepper. Cook for 1 minute. Cook the vegetables: Add the zucchini and tomatoes and toss to combine. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes. Simmer the sauce: Add the chicken stock and Dijon mustard and to incorporate the mustard into the sauce. Bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and add the minced tarragon. Simmer uncovered for 30 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half. Lightly mash the cherry tomatoes as they simmer. Taste and season to your preferences. Cook the pasta: As the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of generously salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook according to package directions. Scoop out ½ cup of the pasta cooking water. Drain the pasta and set aside. Finish the sauce: Add the parsley and lemon juice to the sauce. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes. Taste and season one final time before adding half of the pasta cooking water and the cooked pasta. Toss to coat the pasta in the sauce for 1 to 2 minutes, adding the remaining pasta cooking water if needed. Taste and season. Turn off the heat. To serve: Transfer the pasta and sauce to a large serving platter. Finish with more parsley if desired. Enjoy!
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes

item.title: Memedump + story: An update on the last three insane years.
item.description:
This is the meme that kicked it all off. More memes at the end
A brief history (very brief) of a year's work.
Curve ball half way through.
Now she's over a year old. How time flies.
In the chaos of the last year, people were still reaching out wanting more. So, we just took the dive and did it again. Despite now having a baby to care for and the craziness of the world in general, we took the shot anyway.
GULP
Us busy prepping for the launch (We don't actually have an office - it's just me and Mike at our PCs. But this is what it would look like.)
It launched about a month ago. (Important note: Not my smug baby)
https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/palaeogames/professor-primulas-portfolio-of-palaeontology?ref=9k7w1u
This is a sample of the type of artwork our book has.
Oh, your party only worries about crocs and hags in a swamp environment? Shame.
Here are some example pages from the book.
Chomp chomp.
Our Yi draft design. We're going to adjust it's optional magical rules a little so that it can still poison enemies while being a familiar. Look at him. LOOK AT HIM.
I hope you like the idea of playing as a realistic Neanderthal, as well as dinofolk ofc.
Here's the link if you're interested https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/palaeogames/professor-primulas-portfolio-of-palaeontology?ref=9k7w1u Otherwise, here's some memes to make up for being a shitty ad (sorry).
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sL01P5zsL18&t=1s
Dave Hone at his best (worst).
It's not cannibalism.
Sneaky dnd meme.
If anyone is interested, you can also see our first book here: https://palaeogames.com/ And the Kickstarter here: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/palaeogames/professor-primulas-portfolio-of-palaeontology?ref=9k7w1u
item.dc:creator: Palaeo

item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: Where was Gondor when Theoden was full of shit?
item.description:
The Where was Gondor?-discussion was invented for the film to add drama
In the books Gondor is not blamed, considering Saruman and Gríma had ensured that Théoden would not acknowledge Isengard as a threat until too late
As Éomer, the nephew of Théoden, reported (The Two Towers; III; II): "...there are some, close to the king's ear, that speak craven counsels; but war is coming.... [Saruman's] friends do not all dwell in Isengard. But if you come to the king's house, you shall see for yourself."
The weakened Théoden complied with Gríma's "policy of delay. It was not until his defeat by Gandalf that any action was taken." (Unfinished Tales; III; V)
Therefore Rohan had not alerted Gondor to the peril that grew to the northwest
For the same reason the 10,000 Rohirrim riders had not been mustered (TTT; III; VI): "...Wormtongue played dangerously, always seeking to delay you, to prevent your full strength being gathered. He was crafty: dulling men's wariness, or working on their fears, as served the occasion."
Consequently, the common excuse that Gondor fought on its own borders is true, yet was not why the ally was not aiding Rohan against a threat it was not told about
For the record the beacon system existed "to warn the Rohirrim that Gondor was in danger, or (more rarely) the reverse." (Unfinished Tales; III; II; §35); though as the beacons went unto the border of Rohan they would be lit once the Rohirrim asked for it
At end, for all its popularity the movie quote [that is detrimental to some peoples view on Gondor] shall not be mistaken for lore. Give Gríma and Saruman their due
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: News media differences. Check it out.
item.description:
ABC News says protests nationwide. First article.
CNN says protests across US. First article.
BBC says protests in cities across US. Third article.
Fox had nothing about it. In fact, they had one article that tarrifs (sneaky taxes on the poor) were going to help some Americans. Don't be surprised if Fox viewers get zero info about it, except maybe some misinformation from friends.
item.dc:creator: Panicpeace
